senses.definition: a two-player strategy board game from China, where players move pieces with different abilities across a board to capture their opponent's pieces and ultimately checkmate their opponent's king

examples.x: My grandfather taught me how to play xiangqi, and now we compete every weekend.
Learning xiangqi helped improve my problem-solving skills because the game involves careful strategy.
My dad says xiangqi is his favorite game because it helps him stay sharp and focused.
My friend and I play xiangqi after school because it is fun and makes us think hard.
I watched a video to learn the rules of xiangqi, and now I understand how each piece moves.

senses.definition: a two-player strategy board game from Japan, similar to chess, where players move pieces with different abilities across a board to capture their opponent's pieces and ultimately checkmate their opponent's king

a two-player strategy board game from Korea, similar to xiangqi and shogi, where players move pieces with different abilities across a board to capture their opponent's pieces and ultimately checkmate their opponent's king

examples.x: He spent the afternoon learning how to play janggi with his friends.
After playing chess for years, she decided to try janggi to challenge herself.
The young boy watched a professional janggi tournament on TV, fascinated by the players' skill.

senses.definition: an ancient Indian board game that is believed to be the earliest form of chess

examples.x: Many historians believe that chaturanga was played by royalty in ancient India.
I tried learning chaturanga last weekend, and it's a lot more complex than I expected.
My grandfather told me he used to play chaturanga with his friends when he was young.
We learned about chaturanga in history class, and I didn't realize it was the original version of chess.
I found a chaturanga set in a store today, and it looked very similar to a chess set.

senses.definition: a chess variant where both players race their king to the eighth rank to win the game
a chess variant in which one player, with a larger army of pawns and knights, plays against another player with a regular set of pieces

examples.x: I tried playing Horde chess last weekend, and it was much harder than I expected.
She beat me at Horde chess by capturing all my pieces and then checkmating my king.
I learned about Horde chess online, and now I want to try it with my friends.
If you enjoy regular chess, you might find Horde chess a fun challenge because of the new rules.

senses.definition: a chess variant where captured pieces can be reintroduced into play as one's own

examples.x: We spent hours playing crazyhouse chess last weekend, and I loved the added challenge of using captured pieces.
In crazyhouse chess, you have to plan carefully because the pieces you capture can turn the game around.
I tried playing crazyhouse chess with my friend, and it was a lot more intense than regular chess.
Once I captured his rook in crazyhouse chess, I placed it on the board and used it to set up a checkmate.

senses.definition: a chess variant where the objective is to give the opponent's king three checks, instead of checkmate, to win the game

examples.x: I learned how to play three-check chess at a friend's house last weekend.
We decided to play three-check chess instead of regular chess for a change.
After getting two checks, I was really careful with my moves in three-check chess.
Three-check chess can be more intense than regular chess because of how quickly the game can end.

senses.definition: a variant of chess with faster time controls, where players have limited time to make their moves, typically ranging from a few minutes to a few seconds per move

examples.x: He’s really good at blitz chess because he can make quick decisions under pressure.
We had a quick game of blitz chess before dinner, and it was over in just a few minutes.
I prefer blitz chess over regular chess because it’s faster and more exciting.
She got so caught up in the game that she barely noticed her time running out in the blitz chess match.
They challenged each other to a blitz chess game, seeing who could make the best moves in the shortest time.

senses.definition: a two-player strategy board game from Thailand, similar to chess, where players move pieces with different abilities across a board to capture their opponent's pieces and ultimately checkmate their opponent's king

examples.x: I learned how to play makruk from my Thai friend last year.
After mastering chess, I decided to try makruk for a new challenge.
We spent the afternoon playing makruk at a local café, and I won the final game.
My grandfather used to play makruk with his friends every Sunday afternoon.

senses.definition: a form of chess play where a player does not look at the board but keeps track of the game in their mind, relying solely on their memory and visualization skills to make moves

examples.x: He played blindfold chess at the tournament and impressed everyone with his ability to visualize the board.
I can't imagine playing blindfold chess; it must be so difficult to keep track of all the pieces in your mind.
Blindfold chess is a great way to test your memory and strategic thinking skills.
During the event, grandmasters showcased their skills by playing multiple games of blindfold chess simultaneously.
He trained for weeks, preparing himself for the blindfold chess competition.

senses.definition: a variant of chess played with four players in teams of two, where captured pieces can be passed to a teammate and dropped back onto the board

examples.x: We decided to play bughouse chess at the park yesterday, and it was so much fun!
They spent the afternoon playing bughouse chess at the café, passing pieces back and forth.
He’s really good at bughouse chess, especially when it comes to using his partner's pieces strategically.
We were playing bughouse chess online, and my teammate and I had to adjust our strategies quickly.

senses.definition: a chess variant designed to be played by four players on a square board with additional squares and pieces to accommodate the increased number of players

examples.x: Last weekend, we tried playing four-player chess, and it was much more challenging than regular chess.
My friends and I spent hours playing four-player chess; it was fun but also confusing with so many people involved.
In four-player chess, you have to be careful who you ally with, as the game can change in an instant.
Four-player chess is perfect for game night with friends, as it gets everyone involved and makes the game more unpredictable.

senses.definition: an ancient form of chess that originated in Persia, considered the precursor to modern chess, played on an 8x8 board with different pieces and rules

examples.x: My grandfather taught me how to play shatranj when I was young.
I love learning about the history of shatranj and how it influenced the game of chess.
While shatranj is similar to chess, the movement of the pieces is more limited.
We played a few rounds of shatranj to understand the origins of chess.

senses.definition: a variant of the traditional chess game played on a circular board with unique movement patterns for some pieces

examples.x: My cousin introduced me to circular chess, and it was a fun twist on the classic game we know.
We spent hours playing circular chess and trying to figure out the best way to trap the king.
Circular chess feels like a completely new game because of the way the board is shaped.
The round board in circular chess forces players to think differently about their strategy.
I played circular chess with my friend yesterday, and it was much more challenging than regular chess.

senses.definition: a chess variant that introduces new pieces and changes the initial setup of the traditional chess game

examples.x: I was able to checkmate my opponent quickly in Gothic chess by using the princess to capture his king.
When playing Gothic chess, I realized that the empress is even more powerful than the queen because of its extra movement abilities.
I prefer Gothic chess over regular chess because the new pieces add more strategy and options for planning.
I learned how to use the princess effectively in Gothic chess by positioning it in a way that controls both diagonals and straight lines.

senses.definition: a chess variant where capturing a piece causes an "explosion," removing the capturing piece and all adjacent pieces from the board

examples.x: I lost my bishop in atomic chess because I moved it too close to the opponent's knight, and it exploded after they captured it.
Atomic chess is a fun but tricky variant because it forces you to plan your moves more carefully to avoid self-destructive explosions.
I thought I had the advantage, but atomic chess quickly turned the game around with a sudden explosion.
My strategy in atomic chess was to avoid unnecessary captures that might blow up my best pieces.
When I played atomic chess, I had to think twice before making any captures, knowing it could destroy my own pieces.

senses.definition: a chess variant played on a 10x8 board with two extra pawns per player and new pieces called "Janus" that can move like a bishop or a knight
a chess variant where each player's queen is replaced by a chancellor, a piece that moves like both a rook and a knight

examples.x: I’ve played traditional chess for years, but almost chess with its chancellor piece adds a new challenge.
The chancellor in almost chess made it harder to defend my position because it could move in more directions.
I decided to try almost chess, where the chancellor moves differently than any piece in regular chess.
During our game of almost chess, my opponent used the chancellor to trap my king in a way I didn’t expect.

senses.definition: a variant of chess played on a hexagonal board, where the movement rules are modified to suit the new board shape, creating a unique and different playing experience from traditional chess on a square board

examples.x: I played hexagonal chess for the first time last weekend, and it was a lot more challenging than regular chess.
My friend introduced me to hexagonal chess, and now we play it every Saturday.
I lost quickly in hexagonal chess because I wasn’t used to how the pawns move on the hexagonal board.
Hexagonal chess can be tricky to learn at first, but once you get the hang of it, it becomes really fun.

senses.definition: a large-board variant of chess that uses a 10x10 board and introduces additional pieces and expanded rules, offering new strategic possibilities compared to traditional chess

examples.x: When learning Grand Chess, it takes time to get used to the extra pieces and their unique moves.
Some players enjoy the challenge of Grand Chess because it adds more depth compared to regular chess.
My friend introduced me to Grand Chess last weekend, and I was amazed at how different it felt from regular chess.
I usually play regular chess, but today we decided to try Grand Chess for a change.
I find it harder to win at Grand Chess because the larger board makes it more challenging to control the center.

senses.definition: a traditional chess variant played in Myanmar on a 64-square board with unique pieces and rules distinct from standard chess

examples.x: My uncle is very good at sittuyin; he wins almost every time we play.
We spent the afternoon playing sittuyin with friends and family.
You need to carefully plan your moves in sittuyin, or you might lose quickly.
I learned to play sittuyin from my grandfather when I was young.

senses.definition: a historical chess variant played on a 10x10 board with extra pieces and unique rules, inspired by the historical conqueror Timur

examples.x: Have you ever played Tamerlane chess?
